page_content:
HIST412 History of Women and Gender in Africa (3 Credits) An examination of socio-economic and cultural change in Africa from the dawn of the colonial era in the 19th century to independence in the mid- twentieth century. Major focus on how African women understood and responded to the expansion of European empires, changes in the colonial economy, and impact of westernization and urbanization. Credit Only Granted for: HIST412 or HIST428L. Formerly: HIST428L.

HIST421 Medieval Heresies (3 Credits) An examination of twelfth- and thirteenth-century heresies in the medieval West. Consideration of why so many heretics emerged, and how the church attempted to deal with them, and what effect their persecution had on Europe both then and later. Special attention given to groups that stood on the ﬁne line between heresy and orthodox religion. Credit Only Granted for: HIST408L or HIST421. Formerly: HIST408L.

HIST425 Imperial Russia (3 Credits) The rise and fall of the Russian Empire, Peter the Great to the collapse of tsarism in revolution. Emphasis on the evolution of autocracy, social groups, national identities, and cultural change.
